title: Galaxy Travel Desk: Futuristic Email Exchange (3ESO A2)

questions.prompt: You are a customer writing to Galaxy Travel Desk about a Moon Hotel trip. Which email is best for A2 formal communication?

answers.text: Hi bro, give me moon prices now, bye.
Dear Galaxy Travel Desk, I would like information about your Moon Hotel trip. Kind regards, Alex.
Hello! moon trip?? send details pls!! thanks.

questions.prompt: Match each customer request with the best formal email chunk for Galaxy Travel Desk.

pairs.right: Could you tell me the price for the Mars weekend tour?
I would like to know the available dates for the Time Travel package.
Could you send me more information about the Moon Hotel?
Please let me know your cancellation and return policy.

questions.prompt: A customer wants to book a Space Cruise for July. Which option is the best email start?

answers.text: Subject: Hey trip. Hello, I want space now.
Subject: Booking enquiry, Space Cruise July. Dear Galaxy Travel Desk, I would like to book a Space Cruise in July.
Subject: URGENT. Send me all prices today!!!
Subject: Question. Hi, maybe I go maybe not.

questions.prompt: Dear Galaxy Travel Desk, I would like _____ information about your Time Travel Weekend package. Could you tell me _____ the available dates in August? Kind regards, Lea.
Hi Galaxy Travel, give me the cheapest Mars trip now and answer fast.
questions.corrected: Dear Galaxy Travel Desk, could you please tell me the price of your cheapest Mars trip? I look forward to your reply.

questions.prompt: You are Galaxy Travel Desk. Write one short email sentence to confirm the customer’s Space Cruise booking for 14 August and thank them.
accepted: your space cruise booking for 14 august is confirmed thank you
we confirm your booking for the space cruise on 14 august thank you
your booking is confirmed for 14 august thank you for choosing galaxy travel

questions.prompt: A customer writes: I cannot find my booking code for the Moon Hotel trip. Which reply is best?

answers.text: Dear customer, thank you for your message. Please send your full name and travel date, and we will help you find your booking code.
You are wrong, the code is in your email.

questions.prompt: You are a customer. Write a short email to ask for three things about the Time Travel Weekend trip: price, available dates, and if meals are included. Include greeting and sign-off.

Dear Alex, thank you for your email. Your Space Cruise booking for 14 August is _____. The total price is _____ credits. Please send your passport number to complete your reservation. Kind regards, Galaxy Travel Desk.
Match each customer message with the best next action from Galaxy Travel Desk.

pairs.left: I want to change my travel date, please.
pairs.right: Please tell us your booking code and new preferred date.

pairs.left: I did not receive confirmation email.
pairs.right: We will resend your confirmation now and verify your email address.

pairs.left: Is insurance included in the Moon Hotel package?
pairs.right: Please see the insurance options attached and choose one.

pairs.left: I want to cancel my reservation.
pairs.right: Please review our cancellation policy and confirm if you want to proceed.

questions.prompt: You are Galaxy Travel Desk. A customer asks for a Time Travel Weekend in August. Speak for 20-30 seconds and include greeting, one available date, one price, and one closing line.

Write a short two-message exchange. Message 1: customer asks to book a Moon Hotel trip for September and asks if breakfast is included. Message 2: travel agent replies with confirmation steps and one polite closing.
You are the customer. Speak for 25-35 seconds to ask Galaxy Travel Desk about a Space Vacation package. Include destination, travel month, budget limit, and one special request.
Which closing sentence is best for a formal customer email to Galaxy Travel Desk?

answers.text: Thank you for your help. I look forward to your reply. Kind regards, Nora.
